task_completion.description: Create the required layers with correct colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD should show three layers: 'COMPONENTS' (Cyan), 'BEAM' (Red), and 'LABELS' (White or Black depending on background).

task_completion.description: Draw the laser, mirrors, lens, and detector components
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cyan geometric shapes should be visible: a rectangle on the bottom left (Laser), a diagonal line on the bottom right (M1), a diagonal line on the top right (M2), a vertical ellipse in the top middle (Lens), and a square on the top left (Detector).

task_completion.description: Draw the Z-fold beam path connecting the components
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red line forming a Z-shape should connect the components: starting at the bottom-left rectangle, moving horizontally to the bottom-right line, vertically to the top-right line, and horizontally to the top-left square (passing through the ellipse).

task_completion.description: Add text labels for the components
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels 'LASER', 'M1', 'M2', and 'DETECTOR' should be visible near their respective components.

task_completion.description: Add a note with the calculated total path length
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text stating exactly 'Total Path: 800 mm' should be visible in the drawing area.
The LibreCAD window title bar or a save dialog should display the filename 'optical_setup.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreCAD application interface being actively used, with toolbars, drawing area, and command line visible.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or text editor to generate the DXF file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen manually creating layers, drawing shapes, and adding text in LibreCAD, rather than writing code in a terminal or text editor to output the DXF.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct total beam path length is 800 mm (calculated as 300mm + 200mm + 300mm). The layers must be named 'COMPONENTS' (Cyan), 'BEAM' (Red), and 'LABELS' (White). The beam path forms a Z-shape connecting (0,0) -> (300,0) -> (300,200) -> (0,200).
